title: Guardian annual DBL/PFL prepaid amortization
goal: Verify that the assistant recognizes the annual prepaid pattern before labeling the item unexplained.
prompt_summary: Guardian annual DBL/PFL invoice of 2,559.50 with 2,080.54 current-year premium, 478.96 prior-year true-up, and a 346.76 prepaid-related entry at 2/28.
must_include: split the invoice into prior-year true-up and current-year prepaid
item is explained rather than unexplained
must_not_include: escalation recommended before testing prepaid logic

title: Annualized payroll vs invoice rate validation
goal: Verify that the assistant uses the annual method instead of a monthly divisor conversion.
prompt_summary: Per-pay withholding 489.48 biweekly and monthly carrier rate 1,060.55.
must_not_include: monthly shortfall caused by divisor conversion

title: Mixed medical and non-medical clearing activity
goal: Verify that the assistant separates non-medical products before the medical tieout.
prompt_summary: Anthem medical and Guardian non-medical products both hit the same clearing account.
must_include: identify Guardian or other non-medical items
must_not_include: complete medical tieout before separating mixed activity

title: Three-pay-period month timing variance
goal: Verify that the assistant treats excess withholdings in a three-pay month as an expected timing difference.
prompt_summary: Biweekly payroll month with three payroll runs and an overcredit close to one extra payroll withholding.
must_include: confirm reversal in the next month
must_not_include: structural error before testing the documented timing pattern

title: Termination with pending carrier credit
goal: Verify that the assistant recognizes the pending credit pattern and sets follow-up timing correctly.
prompt_summary: Employee termination mid-month, carrier invoice already paid, payroll withholdings stop, debit remains in clearing.
must_include: expense only after documented review if no credit arrives
must_not_include: immediate unexplained-variance escalation without testing the pending credit pattern
